serviceDescription: Amazon FSx for OpenZFS provides fully managed shared file storage built on the OpenZFS file system and accessible through the NFS protocol (v3, v4, v4.1, and v4.2).

templates.title: Single-AZ (non-HA) Deployment - SSD
templates.description: Supports NVMe read caching, up to 10 GB/s of throughput capacity, and up to 350,000 IOPS

cards.title: Storage settings
cards.helpText: You pay for the average amount of storage provisioned for your file systems per month, measured in gigabyte-months "GB-months".

components.label: Desired storage capacity
components.description: Average amount of data in your file systems per month

components.label: Savings from compression
components.description: SSD storage and backups support data compression. Enter the % of savings you expect with compression.

components.variableName: Savings from compression factor
Effective Storage Monthly
Total cost for FSx for OpenZFS storage

components.label: Total monthly cost for FSx for OpenZFS file system storage

cards.title: IOPS, Throughput
cards.helpText:
Throughput Capacity: You pay for the average throughput capacity you/'ve provisioned for your file systems per month, measured in "MBps-months."

SSD IOPS: By default, three IOPS are included for every GB of SSD storage. You can optionally provision a higher level of IOPS, and you pay for the average IOPS provisioned above your included rate for the month, measured in "IOPS-months".

components.label: Desired aggregate throughput
components.placeholder: Enter the aggregate throughput capacity you want to provision.
components.description: Enter the aggregate throughput capacity you want to provision. Each Amazon FSx for OpenZFS file system supports 160, 320, 640, 1280, 2560, 3840, 5120, 7680, or 10240 MB/s of throughput capacity.

components.label: Desired aggregate IOPS
components.description: Enter the amount of aggregate SSD IOPS you would like to provision. FSx for OpenZFS includes 3 IOPS per GiB of SSD storage, up to the maximum supported by each file system's configured throughput capacity. The estimate will calculate the difference between your desired aggregate SSD IOPS and the default IOPS included based on your aggregate SSD storage.

cards.title: Backup Storage
cards.helpText:
Throughput Capacity: You pay for the average throughput capacity you/'ve provisioned for your file systems per month, measured in "MBps-months."

SSD IOPS: By default, three IOPS are included for every GB of SSD storage. You can optionally provision a higher level of IOPS, and you pay for the average IOPS provisioned above your included rate for the month, measured in "IOPS-months".

components.label: Backup storage
components.placeholder: Enter the amount of backup storage
components.description: Enter the average amount of backup storage consumed for your file systems each month. As backups are incremental, you can generally expect for your backup storage to be roughly equal to the total amount of data, plus the change rate of your data. For example, if you expect to store 100GB of data each month and your data changes by 5GB each month, you should enter 105 GB.

components.outputUnitLabel: GB per month (Effective backup storage capacity)

components.variableName: Monthly cost for FSx for OpenZFS backup storage

operands.variableLabel: GB per month (Effective backup storage capacity)

cards.title: IOPS, Throughput
cards.helpText:
Throughput Capacity: You pay for the average throughput capacity you/'ve provisioned for your file systems per month, measured in "MBps-months."

SSD IOPS: By default, three IOPS are included for every GB of SSD storage. You can optionally provision a higher level of IOPS, and you pay for the average IOPS provisioned above your included rate for the month, measured in "IOPS-months".

components.label: Desired aggregate throughput
components.placeholder: Enter the aggregate throughput capacity you want to provision.
components.description: Enter the aggregate throughput capacity you want to provision. Each Amazon FSx for OpenZFS file system supports 160, 320, 640, 1280, 2560, 3840, 5120, 7680, or 10240 MB/s of throughput capacity.

components.label: SSD Read Cache
components.description: You can optionally provision an SSD read cache to provide lower-latency reads of recently accessed data. By default, FSx chooses a read cache size based on your provisional throughput. If you know the approximate size of your active working data set, you can customize the size of the SSD read cache to fit this active working set. If your workload is not latency-sensitive, you can choose to have no SSD cache to reduce costs.

components.label: info alert
components.content: 'Proportional to Throughput' comes with 5 GB per MBps throughput

components.label: info alert
components.content: With FSx Intelligent Tiering, you do not need to provision IOPS. Instead, you pay for data access based on usage, in the form of read and write requests. A request is a single read or write under operation between the FSx for OpenZFS file system and the FSx Intelligent-Tiering storage layer. FSx for Open ZFS automatically optimizes the conversion between read and write IO to the file system and requests to optimize performance and reduce costs.
